subject: UX & Research
title: The iPhone and Multi-Touch
description: A short lesson to help you apply The iPhone and Multi-Touch.

hook.text: On January 9, 2007, Steve Jobs touched the screen and scrolled through contacts with his finger. The crowd gasped.

buildup.text: Before the iPhone, smartphones had physical keyboards. BlackBerry, Palm, and Nokia ruled with tiny keys.

discovery.text: Multi-touch made the screen the interface. Pinch to zoom, swipe to scroll, tap to select. No stylus, no keys.

twist.text: Microsoft CEO Steve Ballmer laughed at the iPhone. 'No keyboard? Not a good email machine.' He was spectacularly wrong.

climax.text: By 2010, touch had killed the physical keyboard. The iPhone didn't just change phones — it changed computing.

punchline.text: Touch replaced keys. The iPhone redefined how humans interact with machines.

quiz.question: What made the iPhone's interface revolutionary compared to previous smartphones?
options: Multi-touch replaced physical keyboards with direct screen manipulation
It had a larger battery than competitors
It was the first phone with a color screen
